design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate level maze seeking keys/switches to reach boss, defeat boss to progress to next episode. Manage resource scarcity (ammo, health) while engaging enemies in combat with dynamic weapon selection.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity vs. enemy density; exploration for keys/secrets vs. environmental hazards; offensive positioning vs. enemy use of cover and environment.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Undead gunslinger seeking revenge; mastery of an eclectic arsenal (flare gun, voodoo doll, aerosol flamethrower); discovery of hidden areas and secret weapons.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising difficulty despite creative level design; emphasis on exploration rewards (super secret areas); irreverent horror-comedy tone with extensive pop-culture references and anachronistic setting blending 1920s-30s, weird West, and futuristic elements; creative weapon design with secondary attack modes as standard feature; 'Guns Akimbo' dual-wield mechanic as power-up fantasy fulfillment.

difficulty.lives_system: Health-based with healing items; no explicit lives limit in campaign
difficulty.checkpointing: Episode-based progression; level completion required to advance; no mid-level checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Skill-based execution required; players must master weapon switching and positioning; exploration of secret areas provides resource advantage but is optional; enemy AI uses environmental cover dynamically, requiring player adaptation

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition through pickup; temporary power-ups (Guns Akimbo for dual-wield); health/ammo replenishment
progression.unlock_mechanism: Keys required to progress through levels; combination lock doors gate progression; episode completion unlocks next episode
replayability_hooks: Super secret areas with hidden rewards encourage exploration and replay
Multiple difficulty settings (implied by reception criticism)
Level design complexity supports different tactical approaches

world_building_techniques: Backstory relegated to Monolith website and readme documents (not in-game)
Environmental storytelling through anachronistic setting mixing 1920s-40s, weird West, futuristic tech
Level themes reflect occult/horror aesthetic with demonic enemies and cult iconography
Caleb's characterization as merciless gunfighter and jilted cult commander established outside game
Pop-culture references woven throughout create comedic world-building

boss_design.philosophy: Capstone to each episode's revenge quest; serve as narrative checkpoints and skill gates requiring mastery of accumulated weapons and tactics
notable_bosses: Cheogh (gargoyle minion - quest objective across episodes)

faction_indicators: The Cabal - cult of Tchernobog worshippers, Caleb's former organization
The Chosen - highest circle of the Cabal, betrayed by Tchernobog
Human cult members vs. demonic creatures - environmental alliance markers
danger_signals: Gargoyle architecture and religious corruption imagery
Enemy variety reflects power hierarchy (nuisance creatures vs. cabal members vs. boss minions)
environmental_storytelling: Level design reflects different eras (1920s speakeasy, 1930s industrial, retro-futuristic bases, weird West saloons)
Cabal cult iconography and demon worship altars in level geometry
Environmental hazards (crushing blocks, lava pits, teleporters) reinforce supernatural/chaotic setting
Enemy placements suggest cult ritual spaces and demonic lairs
